content.full_text:
California 
Fair Political 
Practices Commission 
June 21, 1989 
Lindi Kacic, Treasurer 
Marian Bergeson For Lt. Governor 
P. O. BOX 3151 
Newport Beach, California 92663 
Dear Ms. Kacic: 
Re: 
Your Request For Advice 
Our File No. A-89-312 
You have requested written confirmation of telephone advice 
provided to you regarding the campaign provisions of the Political 
Reform Act (the "Act).Y 
I advised you that the photographs which were purchased in 
February by Senator Bergeson's Senate campaign committee could be 
used by Senator Bergeson's Lt. Governor campaign committee. 
In 
addition, I advised you that the Lt. Governor's campaign account 
must pay the Senate account the fair market value of the 
photographs. 
My telephone advice was provided prior to the May 15, 1989 
ruling in Service Employees International Union v. Fair Political 
Practices Commission, U.S. District Court, Eastern Dist. of 
California, No. CIVS-89-0433 LKK-JFM. 
In that case, the court 
granted a preliminary injunction limiting enforcement of certain 
provisions of Proposition 73. 
As a result of that ruling, there 
is no restriction on a candidate's ability to transfer campaign 
funds, including assets, among his or her own controlled 
committees. 
lIGovernment Code sections 81000-91015. 
All statutory 
references are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated. 
Commission regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations 
section 18000, et seq. 
All references to regulations are to Title 
2, Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations. 
428 J Street, Suite 800 • 
P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804~0807 • 
(916)322~5660 


Lindi Kacic 
June 21, 1989 
Page 2 
Therefore, campaign funds and assets (including the 
photographs) owned by one of Senator Bergeson's controlled 
committees may be transferred to one of her other controlled 
committees. 
It is not necessary for one committee to pay the 
other committee for assets which are transferred. 
Please call me at (916) 322-5662 if you have additional 
questions. 
KED:AH:ssa 
Sincerely, 
Kathryn E. Donovan 
General Counsel 
(/L~Xi£gAe4J 
By: 
Alice Hughes 
Political Reform Consultant 


Ms. Alice Hughes 
Political Reform Consultant 
Marian Bergeson For Lt. Governor 
P. O. Box 3151 
Newport Beach, CA 92663 
May 9, 1989 
Fair Political Practices Commission 
P. O. Box 807 
Sacramento, CA 95804-0807 
Dear Alice: 
Per our recent conversation, this letter is to confirm 
your telephone advice regarding Sen. Bergeson's Lt. Governor 
campaign using photographs of the Senator that were purchased 
by her Senate administrative account. 
In our telephone conversation on May 4,1989, the same day 
Sen. Bergeson announced she was running for Lt. Governor, her 
new Lt. Governor campaign committee was given approval by the 
FPPC to use photographs of her that were purchased in February 
by her Senate administrative funds. 
We were also advised by 
you that the Lt. Governor campaign account must reimburse the 
Senate account for the cost of the photos. 
If there are any additions or corrections to this letter, 
please let me know. 
Sincerely, 
Lindi Kacic, Treasurer 


Ms. Alice Hughes 
Political Reform Consultant 
Marian Bergeson For Lt. Governor 
P. O. Box 3151 
Newport Beach, CA 92663 
May 9, 1989 
Fair Political Practices Commission 
P. O. Box 807 
Sacramento, CA 95804-0807 
Dear Alice: 
Per our recent conversation, this letter is to confirm 
your telephone advice regarding Sen. Bergeson's Lt. Governor 
campaign using photographs of the Senator that were purchased 
by her Senate administrative account. 
In our telephone conversation on May 4,1989, the same day 
Sen. Bergeson announced she was running for Lt. Governor, her 
new Lt. Governor campaign committee was given approval by the 
FPPC to use photographs of her that were purchased in February 
by her Senate administrative funds. 
We were also advised by 
you that the Lt. Governor campaign account must reimburse the 
Senate account for the cost of the photos. 
If there are any additions or corrections to this letter, 
please let me know. 
Sincerely, 
~~~~ 
Lindi Kacic, Treasurer
